policy: capability parity is required; invocation syntax may differ by host

capabilities.purpose: Apply the Hakim decision ladder in lite, full, ultra, or off mode.

claude-code.invocation: /hakim:full for explicit user invocation; hidden canonical skill remains model-invocable

github-copilot.invocation: use the installed Hakim skill `hakim`, or matching natural-language activation

opencode.invocation: /hakim <lite|full|ultra|off> or the OpenCode native skill tool

capabilities.purpose: Review a diff for unnecessary complexity and produce a delete or simplify list.

github-copilot.invocation: use the installed `hakim-review` skill or select the `hakim-reviewer` custom agent

opencode.invocation: /hakim-review or the OpenCode native skill tool

capabilities.purpose: Manually audit an explicitly inspected repository scope for evidence-backed removable complexity and native or standard-library alternatives.

github-copilot.invocation: use the installed `hakim-audit` skill or select the `hakim-auditor` custom agent

opencode.invocation: /hakim-audit or the OpenCode native skill tool

capabilities.purpose: Harvest deliberate Hakim shortcuts and validate debt-ledger evidence without inventing live debt.

github-copilot.invocation: use the installed `hakim-debt` skill or select the `hakim-debt-analyst` custom agent

opencode.invocation: /hakim-debt or the OpenCode native skill tool

capabilities.purpose: Show the accepted Hakim evidence-status card without unsupported performance or ROI numbers.

github-copilot.invocation: use the installed `hakim-gain` skill or select the `hakim-evidence-verifier` custom agent

opencode.invocation: /hakim-gain or the OpenCode native skill tool

capabilities.purpose: Show a host-aware quick reference for Hakim modes, capabilities, evidence boundaries, and validation commands.

opencode.invocation: /hakim-help or the OpenCode native skill tool
